fact_claims.claim: OpenClaw memory-wiki is enabled in bridge mode with auto compile, daily note indexing, memory root indexing, dream report indexing, and shared search across corpus all.

evidence_refs.note: Daily note recording memory-wiki enablement details.
Wiki vault is active and currently reporting no contradictions.

fact_claims.claim: Layer 3 is currently a hybrid durable operational memory using Wiki.js plus disk memory files and bridge indexing, while layer 4 is early-stage reflective and procedural memory driven by dreaming and nightly reconciliation rather than a fully structured procedural engine.

evidence_refs.note: Daily note explicitly describing current layer 3 and layer 4 implementation status.
Dream/recall events repeatedly reference the memory layer implementation map.

reflections.observation: The wiki vault is running in bridge mode, but zero exported artifacts are currently available from the memory plugin.
reflections.pattern: Bridge indexing can be configured correctly while still lacking public artifact exports, which limits cross-surface provenance.
reflections.recommended_action: Treat current bridge results as partial and only promote wiki-backed memory updates that are directly supported by workspace evidence until export artifacts appear.

evidence_refs.note: Direct wiki status warning from this maintenance run.

procedures.name: Nightly memory and wiki reconcile loop
procedures.purpose: Run the ordered nightly reconciliation flow across daily memory, dreams, structured SQLite memory, and wiki state without silently canonizing risky claims.
preconditions: Recent daily memory notes or workspace artifacts exist
steps: Gather recent evidence from daily memory files, dream outputs, relevant workspace memory artifacts, and useful wiki changes.
Generate a candidate payload of fact claims, reflections, and procedures with evidence refs.
Ingest the candidate payload into structured-memory/memory.db.
Run wiki and memory lint or reconcile checks, then inspect contradictions, open questions, and provenance gaps.
Update durable wiki-backed operational memory only for low-risk, well-supported items.
Notify Laurynas only when there is a meaningful result, contradiction, approval-needed issue, or blocker.
expected_outcomes: Structured memory gains evidence-backed candidate or verified records.
Wiki reports stay reconciled with current evidence.
Risky domains remain suggestion-based rather than silently canonized.
rollback: Remove or downgrade inserted records whose evidence is weak or contradictory.
Skip wiki updates when support is incomplete.
safety_notes: Keep secrets out of payloads and outputs.
Do not auto-promote risky security or access-control claims without strong direct evidence.

evidence_refs.note: Daily note records dreaming, bridge mode, and nightly maintenance enablement.
Existing ingestion helper supports the candidate payload workflow.
